rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical cyclopropanation of cinnamaldehydes with dimethyl malonate via iminium/enamine organocatalysis combined with iodide electrocatalytic mediation
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of iodide to iodine radical (E-cat cycle) and anodic oxidation of the enamine intermediate (ii) to a radical cation that captures the iodine radical; cathode reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: iminium ion from condensation of pyrrolidine catalyst with cinnamaldehyde 1a (i)
enamine adduct after Michael addition of dimethyl malonate (ii)
enamine-derived radical cation captured by I• (ii-a/iii)
cyclopropane-fused iminium (iv) after intramolecular SN2 displacement of iodide
iodine radical (I•) generated at the anode

electron_flow: At the anode, iodide is oxidized to I• (E-cat cycle); in parallel the enamine intermediate (ii) is anodically oxidized to a radical cation that couples with I• to give an α-iodo iminium (iii). Intramolecular displacement of iodide by the malonate carbon closes the cyclopropane (iv); hydrolysis releases product 3a and regenerates the pyrrolidine catalyst, while the cathode reduces protons to H2.
notes: This scheme (Scheme S2) is presented as an ALTERNATIVE mechanism to the main proposal: instead of α-iodination of the enamine by I• directly, the enamine is first oxidized anodically to a radical cation which then captures I•. Both pathways converge on the α-iodo iminium (iii) and subsequent intramolecular cyclopropanation. No probe experiments (TEMPO, KIE, CV, DFT) are shown on this page to distinguish the two.
